title: 5 stocks with consistent score improvement and upside potential of up to 26% in 1 year, according to analysts
summary: Here are three questions. First: When will the war end? Second: How long will it take to rebuild destroyed infrastructure? Third: How will the geopolitical situation pan out after? The answers to all three are up in the air. So, there is really no point in trying to figure out what will happen to the global equity markets. There is, however, one fact to consider: The market is in the habit of sharply pricing in events. And the short covering it witnessed on Thursday was perhaps the first indication of what is in the store. Our selected stocks for today depict a strong upward trajectory in their overall average score which is based on five key pillars: Earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum. This implies that there has been a significant improvement in their market outlook in the given time frame.

title: Sensex SIP turns  ₹25 lakh into  ₹1.54 crore over 25 years despite multiple market shocks
summary: The BSE Sensex, which stood at 3,262 in 2001, surged sharply in the following years, reaching 20,286 by early 2008. Even during the COVID-hit year of 2020, the index ended with around 16% gains

title: Rights issues hit 5-year high in FY26 amid market volatility
summary: Promoter-backed funding gains traction as tariff tensions, war jitters and FPI outflows reshape capital-raising choices

title: Stocks to buy under  ₹200: Mehul Kothari recommends three shares to buy or sell
summary: Stocks to buy under  ₹100: Mehul Kothari recommends three shares to buy or sell — UCO Bank, NBCC, and PNB. Also shares buying and profit-booking levels

title: Swimwear demand surges with rise in fitness swimming and domestic travel
summary: The swimwear market has reached  ₹8.5 billion, fuelled by a rise in swimming for fitness and a shift toward domestic travel due to the West Asia war. While leader Speedo leans on performance heritage, D2C startups are eating into its market share.

title: Why India is becoming a key market for mid-luxury Swiss watches
summary: Swiss luxury watchmaker Tag Heuer plans to grow its presence in India, particularly in major cities, due to increasing demand for premium watches. The brand sees India becoming a key market in the EMEA region while remaining focused on mid-luxury offerings.

title: Telecom regulator orders Reliance Jio to stop ‘discriminatory’ tariff practices
summary: The directive follows a probe into Jio’s discontinuation of certain entry-level prepaid recharge plans offering 1 GB data per day, which the operator had said were available only through its own retail stores.
